name: PyMuPDF
what_it_does: Fast Python binding for the MuPDF C library that extracts text, images, and annotations from PDF, XPS, EPUB, and other document formats.
use_cases: Bulk text extraction from thousands of PDFs with high throughput
Extracting images and embedded media from PDF documents
Reading and writing PDF annotations and form fields
Rendering PDF pages to images (PNG, JPEG) at arbitrary DPI
Splitting, merging, and reordering PDF pages programmatically
not_for: Extracting tables with structural layout (use tabula-py or camelot instead)
Pure-Python environments without binary dependency support
Proprietary closed-source applications without a commercial MuPDF license (AGPL restriction)
best_when: You need the fastest available Python PDF text or image extraction and can accept the AGPL license or purchase a commercial license.
avoid_when: Your distribution pipeline forbids AGPL dependencies or you need structured table extraction rather than raw text.

auth.notes: Local Python library — no authentication required

pricing.paid_tiers: Commercial license available from Artifex for proprietary use
pricing.notes: Dual-licensed: AGPL v3 (free/open-source) and commercial license from Artifex. AGPL requires that any application distributing PyMuPDF also open-source its code.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Raises RuntimeError with descriptive messages for corrupt or encrypted PDFs; exception types are not always specific enough to distinguish error classes
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All read operations are fully idempotent; write operations overwrite the target file deterministically
known_agent_gotchas: AGPL license may block use in proprietary agent pipelines without a commercial license
fitz is the legacy import name — agents must `import fitz` not `import pymupdf` on older installs
Password-protected PDFs raise an exception unless authenticate() is called first
Page coordinates use bottom-left origin (PDF convention), not top-left, which surprises most developers
Large PDFs with many images can exhaust memory if pages are not explicitly closed after processing

security_score_components.security_notes: No network layer; MuPDF C library has had historical CVEs — keep PyMuPDF updated; process only trusted PDF sources in agent pipelines
